chunk_summary: This section discusses macronutrient requirements for toddlers aged 1-3 years, emphasizing age-appropriate dietary guidelines, nutrient balance, and the prevention of non-communicable diseases through proper nutrition and reduced junk food consumption.
learning_objectives: Understand the macronutrient needs of toddlers aged 1-3 years.
Learn the recommended dietary guidelines for toddlers as per ICMR-NIN and WHO.
Identify strategies to prevent non-communicable diseases through balanced nutrition.
Recognize the impact of junk food consumption on toddler health.
key_facts: Toddlers aged 1-3 years require approximately 1,000-1,300 kcal/day, depending on activity levels (ICMR-NIN, 2024).
Protein intake should be around 13-15 grams/day for optimal growth and development.
Fat should contribute 30-40% of total energy intake, with a focus on healthy fats.
Carbohydrates should make up 50-60% of total energy intake, prioritizing complex carbohydrates.
Excessive consumption of junk food is linked to obesity and non-communicable diseases in children (Indian Pediatrics, 2019).
reference_quote: "Excessive consumption of junk food is a major contributor to the rising prevalence of obesity and non-communicable diseases in children." (Indian Pediatrics, 2019)
document_key_topics: Macronutrient requirements for toddlers aged 1-3 years
Prevention of non-communicable diseases in early childhood
Impact of junk food on toddler health
